name: Bean Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
Revised after testing February 1994. Used 1 1/2 cups flour, not 2 1/2 cups as printed in the book. The 1 cup beans probably replaced 1 cup flour in the original recipe.
1. Stir dry ingredients together in a mixing bowl.
2. Cut in butter until mixture resembles crumbs.
3. In a food processor or blender, puree beans with milk. Alternatively, mash beans and stir in milk.
4. Stir the bean-milk mixture into the dry ingredients all at once to make a light, soft dough.
5. Turn out onto a lightly floured board. Sprinkle with flour and knead about 20 seconds.
6. Shape biscuits by rolling, patting, or cutting; alternatively, divide dough into 16 pieces and shape by hand.
7. Bake at 400°F for 12 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
